status: internal commercial posture patch, not buyer-ready final copy
reason: Skeptical diligence review found that upper-tier pricing and licensing language is ahead of evidence before a completed referenceable pilot.
external_positioning: SpecShift Architectural Logic Diagnostics is an early-stage diagnostic framework for surfacing architectural failure patterns in agentic workflows before broader rollout.

first_outreach_offer.name: Focused Diagnostic Pilot
first_outreach_offer.scope: One concrete AI-agent workflow, reviewed against a narrow set of relevant failure patterns.
first_outreach_offer.price_language: Pricing discussed after scoping. Do not include $750k-$1.5M or $3M-$5M+ tiers in first outreach.
deliverables: Handoff, evidence, escalation, and recovery-path gap list
first_outreach_offer.boundary: Pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review only. Not benchmark, certification, compliance audit, runtime monitor, security tool, deployment approval, or guarantee of AI safety.

business_continuity_stub: SpecShift should be prepared to explain continuity in plain terms: pilot scope is documented, deliverables are file-based and version-controlled, and any engagement would include milestone-based delivery and a defined off-ramp if continuity risk affects performance.
claim_boundary: This posture fails if first outreach leads with upper-tier license pricing, implies external validation, or presents internal artifact completeness as market validation.
next_action: Patch outreach and strategic package references so first-contact materials lead with one focused diagnostic pilot and reserve upper-tier pricing for later qualified conversations.
